creator: kena@vodka-pomme.net
text:
Using a gmirror/gjournal filesystem on 2 external USB drives, the gmirror device is not detected during boot if the drives are connected prior to booting.

It should.

It is detected correctly if the USB drives are connected *after* the boot has completed (after "login" starts).

Configuration (when all is fine):

umass0: <NEC Electronics uPD720133, class 0/0, rev 2.00/0.00, addr 2> on uhub3
umass1: <NEC Electronics uPD720133, class 0/0, rev 2.00/0.00, addr 3> on uhub3
da0 at umass-sim1 bus 1 target 0 lun 0
da0: <SAMSUNG SP1604N TM10> Fixed Direct Access SCSI-2 device 
da0: 40.000MB/s transfers
da0: 152627MB (312581808 512 byte sectors: 255H 63S/T 19457C)
da1 at umass-sim0 bus 0 target 0 lun 0
da1: <SAMSUNG SP1604N TM10> Fixed Direct Access SCSI-2 device 
da1: 40.000MB/s transfers
da1: 152627MB (312581808 512 byte sectors: 255H 63S/T 19457C)

# glabel status
      Name  Status  Components
ufs/dumpty     N/A  da0s2a
ufs/humpty     N/A  da1s2a
    ufs/ao     N/A  mirror/cxm.journal

# gjournal status
              Name  Status  Components
mirror/cxm.journal     N/A  mirror/cxm

# gmirror status
      Name    Status  Components
mirror/cxm  COMPLETE  da0s1
                      da1s1


dmesg report when booting the system with the drives connected prior to boot:

GEOM_LABEL: Label for provider da0s2a is ufs/humpty.
GEOM_LABEL: Label for provider da1s2a is ufs/dumpty.
Trying to mount root from ufs:/dev/ad0s1a


At this point the system is up, the two "regular" UFS filesystems are mounted, but the device mirror/cxm has not been enabled:

# gmirror status
(no output)

When using "gmirror unload; gmirror load" nothing happens (i.e. no disk activity, no further dmesg output, no new devices)

After unmounting da0s2a and da1s2a and unplugging/replugging the drives to the system, dmesg reports the following additional messages:

umass1: at uhub3 port 4 (addr 3) disconnected
(da1:umass-sim1:1:0:0): lost device
(da1:umass-sim1:1:0:0): removing device entry
GEOM_LABEL: Label ufs/dumpty removed.
umass1: detached
umass0: at uhub3 port 2 (addr 2) disconnected
(da0:umass-sim0:0:0:0): lost device
(da0:umass-sim0:0:0:0): removing device entry
GEOM_LABEL: Label ufs/humpty removed.
umass0: detached
umass0: <NEC Electronics uPD720133, class 0/0, rev 2.00/0.00, addr 2> on uhub3
umass1: <NEC Electronics uPD720133, class 0/0, rev 2.00/0.00, addr 3> on uhub3
da0 at umass-sim1 bus 1 target 0 lun 0
da0: <SAMSUNG SP1604N TM10> Fixed Direct Access SCSI-2 device 
da0: 40.000MB/s transfers
da0: 152627MB (312581808 512 byte sectors: 255H 63S/T 19457C)
da1 at umass-sim0 bus 0 target 0 lun 0
da1: <SAMSUNG SP1604N TM10> Fixed Direct Access SCSI-2 device 
da1: 40.000MB/s transfers
da1: 152627MB (312581808 512 byte sectors: 255H 63S/T 19457C)
GEOM_LABEL: Label for provider da0s2a is ufs/dumpty.
GEOM_MIRROR: Device mirror/cxm launched (2/2).
GEOM_JOURNAL: Journal 2557790385: mirror/cxm contains data.
GEOM_JOURNAL: Journal 2557790385: mirror/cxm contains journal.
GEOM_JOURNAL: Journal mirror/cxm clean.
GEOM_LABEL: Label for provider da1s2a is ufs/humpty.
GEOM_LABEL: Label for provider mirror/cxm.journal is ufs/ao.

At this point the device mirror/cxm is ready to use and can be mounted successfully.

Fix: 

Disconnect and reconnect the USB drives.
How-To-Repeat: 1. configure a 2-way mirror on external USB drives
2. reboot the system while keeping the external drives connected (and externally powered)

observe: the gmirror is not detected upon system startup.
